steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The subject is "Analyst" (singular — one word). The phrase "like other dactyls that pair exclusively with one other word" is a parenthetical example; removing it leaves: "'Analyst' ______ with 'panelist' to form a closed rhyming pair."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: couple — base/plural form. "Analyst couple with" — singular subject needs a singular verb, not the base form. Eliminated.

B: are coupled — plural passive. "Analyst are coupled" — "are" disagrees with a singular subject. Eliminated.

C: couples — third-person singular present (-s ending). "'Analyst' couples with 'panelist'" — subject and verb agree. Correct.

D: have coupled — plural auxiliary "have." Same number error as B. Eliminated.
